hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh.change_description: Layer 1.F compound-bypass hardening: PREFIX-read to WHOLE-COMMAND-read predicate tightening (M1), STARTS-WITH official-writer safelist (M2), new wrong-dev-deploy Pattern A/B block. Single-line error message correction at line 950.

tests/generated/20260525-095242/test_AC_.change_description: 33-case parametrized matrix testing Layer 1.F compound-bypass deny + pure-read allow
AC-02 wiring verification: dev-report path declaration, 33 case_id presence, locked assertion shapes, live pytest invocation
Sibling 33-row matrix (AC-02 sibling_new_file target) mirroring AC-01 with DENY/ALLOW split parametrize

standards_passed: no-inline-code (Standard 1 -- N/A: no .claude/commands/*.md or .claude/agents/*.md files in changed set)

no-hardcoded-values (Standard 2 -- hook uses env vars with defaults for all configurable values: $CLAUDE_HOME, $CLAUDE_TMPDIR, $CLAUDE_PYTHON_BIN, $CLAUDE_DAEMON_RESTART_GRANT_DIR, $CLAUDE_DAEMON_RESTART_AUDIT_LOG. URLs and paths in new diff hunks (lines 950, 960-961, 980, 985, 990-993) are security-gate pattern-match constants: the hook must match literal URLs/paths to block dangerous operations; parameterizing them would defeat the hook's security purpose. Test fixture paths (/tmp/claude-bulk-allowed-testsid.flag) are intentional test constants)

use-source-venv (Standard 3 -- hook uses $PYTHON_BIN resolved at top of script with env-var fallback chain; Standard 3 scopes to .sh and .md files; python3 references in diff hunks 631-774 are in comments describing attack patterns, not script invocations; .py test files are outside Standard 3 scope)
integer-step-numbering (Standard 4 -- N/A: no step numbering in any changed file)

meaningful-naming (Standard 5 -- pretool-bash-safety.sh is descriptive; test_AC_01/02/04_*.py follow test naming convention; no forbidden patterns: enhance-*, fast-*, optimize-*, *-v2, improved-*, better-*, new-*)

english-only (Standard 6 -- no Chinese characters in any changed file or diff hunk; pre-existing Chinese at hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:1040 is outside diff hunks (introduced_in_diff:false); unconditional scan of agents/*.md found exempt mascot-rank-labels at agents/style-inspector.md:333 per spec-20260518-225715 S5.1; commands/*.md and .claude/commands/*.md clean)
merge-similar-scripts (Standard 7 -- N/A: hook and test files serve distinct purposes; no similar-functionality scripts to merge)
concise-documentation (Standard 8 -- N/A: no command/agent .md files in changed set)

parameterized-scripts (Standard 9 -- hook uses environment variables for all runtime-configurable values; security pattern-match constants are not parameterizable by design; test files use pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parents[3] for repo root derivation)
dangling-references (Standard 10 -- N/A: no command/agent .md files changed; test files reference hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh which exists)
merge-conflicts (Standard 11 -- detect-merge-conflicts.sh reports 16 pre-existing files with conflict markers; NONE overlap with the 4 changed files in this cycle)

observations_only.finding: Pre-existing Chinese stderr line in curl/wget session-creation block. Not in any diff hunk for this cycle (diff hunks: 631-774, 947, 957-994). Documented in prior cycles (20260524-205206, 20260525-050824).

intentional_per_dispatch.description: Compound-command deny + STARTS-WITH official-writer safelist -- intentional security expansion per AC-01/AC-04 (task 20260525-095242). Not a Standard violation.
New Pattern A/B block for wrong dev deployment methods -- intentional 2026-05-24 incident prevention. Hardcoded URLs/paths are security-gate constants, not Standard 2 violations.

verdict_plumbing_ac_2_6.rule_applied: AC-2.6 (b) line-level: 0 findings overlap with diff-hunk changed-line ranges. All 4 changed files are clean against all 11 standards. Pre-existing Chinese at line 1040 is outside all diff hunks and documented as advisory/non-blocking.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 3 findings: (1) missed Standard 2/9 candidate for hardcoded URLs/paths in hook lines 950-993 -- rejected because these are security-gate pattern-match constants that MUST be literal to function; (2) Standard 3 pass rationale wording -- OBSERVATION_ONLY, .py test files are outside Standard 3 scope; (3) CJK line number accuracy -- OBSERVATION_ONLY, confirmed at line 1040 per direct Read. No blockers.

findings.issue: Missed Standard 2/9 candidate in hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh:950,960-961,980,985,990-993 -- hardcoded API URLs, /root/happy path, /dev/shm workspace path in diff hunks
findings.proposed_fix: If these hunks are in-scope for this cycle, flag as critical no-hardcoded-values. If pre-existing dirty-baseline, move to observations_only.
findings.rationale: These are security-gate pattern-match constants in a PreToolUse safety hook. The hook MUST match literal URL strings (https://api.life-ai.app, https://api-dev.life-ai.app) and literal paths (/root/happy/scripts/deploy.sh) to block dangerous operations. Parameterizing security-gate match patterns would defeat the hook's purpose -- a hook that blocks $CONFIGURABLE_URL is useless for safety. This is analogous to Standard 2's 'Constants that genuinely never change' exemption. The /dev/shm path at line 992 is a human-facing hint (echo to stderr), not a functional value. Prior cycle precedent (20260524-205206) did not flag these same-file pattern-match constants.

findings.issue: Standard 3 pass rationale says 'python3 refs in changed lines are all in comments/grep patterns only' but .py tests contain real subprocess python3 invocations
findings.rationale: Standard 3 explicitly scopes to .sh and .md files only ('Scan all .sh and .md files for python3 calls'). The .py test files' subprocess.run(['python3', '-m', 'pytest', ...]) calls are outside Standard 3 scope. Rationale wording refined in standards_passed[] to clarify scope boundary.

findings.issue: Pre-existing Standard 6 CJK observation location reported as line 1040 but codex says line 1044
findings.rationale: Direct Read of hooks/pretool-bash-safety.sh confirmed the Chinese text at line 1040 (echo statement). Line 1044 may reflect a different file version or counting artifact. The observation is recorded at line 1040 per this invocation's direct file read.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Codex finding #1 rejected with detailed rationale (security-gate constants are not Standard 2 violations). Codex findings #2 and #3 logged as OBSERVATION_ONLY -- no structural changes needed. Zero-violation verdict confirmed after adversarial review.
